questions.text: The diagram shows a tree trunk, with a ring of bark, which includes the phloem removed.The tree will eventually die because this action cuts off the supply of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mineral salts are transported via the xylem, not the phloem.
Organic nutrients, such as sugars, are transported from the leaves to other parts of the plant via the phloem.
Oxygen is not transported through phloem; it is taken up by the roots from the soil.
Water is transported through the xylem, not the phloem.

questions.explanation: Organic nutrients such as sugar molecules are transported through phloem tubes,cutting off this tube cuts off this organic nutrients supply. Moreover, Oxygen supply to the roots is through oxygen dissolved in soil.Mineral salts and water supply to the leaves is through xylem vessels.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from heart. Thus, lymph vessels don't empty into arteries.
Lymph vessels ultimately empty into veins, specifically the subclavian veins, which are located near the base of the neck.
Capillaries are sites of exchange between arteries and veins, not drainage points.
This option is incorrect because option B is correct.

questions.explanation: Lymph drains into subclavian veins, returning lymph to the bloodstream, maintaining fluid balance and immunity.

questions.text: The function of papillary muscles is to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Move blood from semilunar valve into pulmonary vein
options.explanation: Papillary muscles are not involved in moving blood from the semilunar valve into the pulmonary vein.

options.text: Prevent the backward flow of blood from the ventricle
options.explanation: The function of papillary muscles is to prevent the backward flow or regurgitation of blood from the ventricles into the atria.

options.text: Push the blood from right atrium to left atrium
options.explanation: Pressure due to continuous blood entry into the heart and opening and closing of valves pushes blood from right atrium to left atrium and from left atrium to aorta.

options.text: Push the blood from left atrium to aorta
options.explanation: The papillary muscles are not responsible for pushing blood from the left atrium to the aorta

questions.explanation: The papillary muscles are muscles located in the ventricles of the heart. They attach to the cusps of the atrioventricular valves, via the chordae tendineae and contract to prevent inversion or prolapse of these valves on systole.

questions.text: Identify the only cells in leaf epidermis, which are NOT connected to other cells by plasmodesmata and are involved in photosynthesis.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Epidermal cells are connected by plasmodesmata. Epidermal cells are the primary protective outer layer of plant structures, forming a single layer of tightly packed cells that cover the stem, root, leaf, flower, and seed.

Spongy mesophyll cells allows interchange of gases and not part of the epidermis. Spongy mesophyll cells are a type of plant cells found within the mesophyll layer of a leaf. The mesophyll layer is responsible for photosynthesis and are connected by plasmodesmata.

Guard cells are not connected by plasmodesmata and take part in photosynthesis. Guard cells are specialized cells found in the epidermis of plant leaves and stems. They are named so because of their role in regulating the opening and closing of small pores called stomata.
Palisade mesophyll layer is not part of the epidermis. Palisade mesophyll cells are a specialized type of plant cells found in the mesophyll layer of plant leaves.

questions.explanation: Guard cells, containing chloroplast, are a part of the leaf’s epidermal layer ( the epidermis). They, indeed, do not have plasmodesmata (small channels that directly connect the cytoplasm of neighboring plant cells to each other). Spongy and Palisade mesophyll are not part of the epidermal layer and contain Plasmodesmata. Epidermal cells apart from guard cells are not photosynthetic. there is no book line directly refering to this, but since guard cells are not involved in transportation and are mainly responsible for the closing and opening of stomata therefore they donot have plasmodesmata.

questions.text: The massive accumulation of blood within a tissue is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Refers to excessive or uncontrolled bleeding from blood vessels, but it does not specifically describe blood accumulation within a tissue.

Hematoma is generally defined as a collection of blood outside of blood vessels. Most commonly, hematomas are caused by an injury to the wall of a blood vessel, prompting blood to seep out of the blood vessel into the surrounding tissues.
Refers to a liver tumor (hepatic carcinoma) and is unrelated to blood accumulation in tissues.
A body cavity in invertebrates where blood is present, but it is not related to blood accumulation in tissues.

Hematemesis is the medical term for vomiting blood, a symptom of internal bleeding in the upper digestive tract (esophagus, stomach, duodenum) that can appear bright red or look like dark coffee grounds.﻿

questions.explanation: A hematoma is a localized collection of blood outside vessels, usually due to vessel injury.

questions.text: The diagram shows a model to demonstrate the mass flow hypothesis of translocation. In a plant, what are the structures W, X, Y, and Z, and what is the direction of the flow of solution along W?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Since Y represents concentrated sucrose, it means it has higher amount of sucrose. Leaves would have higher amount of sucrose, as sugar is being synthesized and converted into sucrose in leaves. Sucrose is transported through phloem so W represents phloem, Z represents root because direction of flow of sucrose is from source to sink, where source is leaves and sink are roots in this case. So direction of flow is from Y to Z.

So direction of flow is from Y to Z.

So direction of flow is from Y to Z.

Sucrose is transported through phloem so W represents phloem, Z represents root because direction of flow of sucrose is from source to sink, where source is leaves and sink are roots in this case. So direction of flow is from Y to Z.

questions.text: The diagram represents two liquids, separated by a membrane through which osmosis can occur. What movement of molecules will occur?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Molecules of dissolved substance move from left to right
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Osmosis, the spontaneous passage or diffusion of water or other solvents through a semipermeable membrane (one that blocks the passage of dissolved substances—i.e., solutes). Water molecules move from left to right as water potential is higher on the left side then the right side.

options.text: Molecules of dissolved substance move from right to left
options.explanation: This is incorrect.osmosis, the spontaneous passage or diffusion of water or other solvents through a semipermeable membrane (one that blocks the passage of dissolved substances—i.e., solutes). Water molecules move from left to right as water potential is higher on the left side then the right side.

options.text: Overall, water molecules move from left to right
options.explanation: Osmosis, the spontaneous passage or diffusion of water or other solvents through a semipermeable membrane (one that blocks the passage of dissolved substances—i.e., solutes). Water molecules move from left to right as water potential is higher on the left side then the right side.

options.text: Overall, water molecules move from right to left
options.explanation: This is incorrect.osmosis, the spontaneous passage or diffusion of water or other solvents through a semipermeable membrane (one that blocks the passage of dissolved substances—i.e., solutes). Water molecules move from left to right as water potential is higher on the left side then the right side.

questions.explanation: Water molecules move from the left to right as water potential is higher on the left side than the right side.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Atherosclerosis is related to the circulatory system, not the immune system.
Atherosclerosis is the buildup of fats, cholesterol, and other substances in and on the walls of arteries (plaque), which can restrict blood flow. The plaque can burst, triggering a blood clot.
Atherosclerosis specifically affects arteries, not veins.
Atherosclerosis affects blood vessels, not the lungs directly.

questions.explanation: Atherosclerosis is the buildup of plaques (fats, cholesterol) in artery walls, restricting blood flow and risking clot formation.

questions.text: A destarched leaf on a potted plant was covered with Black (A) Transparent (B) strips of paper as shown in figure. After 6 hours of exposure to sunlight the leaf was removed from the plant and tested for starch. Which are the following will be the correct observation?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The portion covered with black strips of paper had no sunlight and hence no photosynthesis, it would not turn blue black after the test. Because sunlight is necessary for photosynthesis and chromoplast which is needed to give colours .

options.text: Only B portion turned blue black
options.explanation: B portion and the area uncovered by any strips turns blue black as it performs photosynthesis

options.text: Only A & B portions remained colourless and the rest of the leaf turned blue black
options.explanation: A portion remains colourless but B is blue black along with the rest of the leaf

options.text: Only A remained colourless and the rest of the leaf turned blue black
options.explanation: A remained colourless due to the blockage of sunlightthe leaf was covered with black (A) strips of paper, it would have blocked sunlight from reaching the leaf. Without sunlight, photosynthesis cannot occur, and the leaf would not produce starch.

questions.explanation: A remained colourless due to the blockage of sunlight the leaf was covered with black (A) strips of paper, it would have blocked sunlight from reaching the leaf. Without sunlight, photosynthesis cannot occur, and the leaf would not produce starch.

questions.text: The main force that generates the blood pressure in vessels is the contraction of the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In the context of blood pressure generation, the atria primarily contribute to the filling of the ventricles, which subsequently affects the volume of blood ejected from the heart with each contraction.

Ventricles of the heart contract to push blood into the arteries and blood pressure is the force exerted by the blood on the walls of the arteries. The ventricles play a vital role in generating blood pressure in vessels.
Arteries can not contract. Their pressure is increased due to the pressure generated by the ventricles of heart. They play a different role in circulatory system.
Veins can not contract. Veins play a different role in the circulatory system compared to arteries and do not actively generate blood pressure in vessels.

questions.explanation: Ventricular contraction ejects blood into the major arteries, resulting in a flow from regions of higher pressure to regions of lower pressure, as blood encounters smaller arteries and arterioles, then capillaries, then the venules and veins of the venous system.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Blood pressure is the pressure exerted by the blood on the walls of the blood vessels. The aorta is the largest artery of the body that carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle of the heart to the rest of the body. Blood pressure is highest in the aorta because this is the location where the blood is ejected from the heart with the greatest force. As the blood moves away from the heart, the blood pressure gradually decreases

Blood pressure is not highest in veins. Veins are blood vessels that carry deoxygenated blood back to the heart from various parts of the body. The pressure in veins is relatively low compared to arteries because the blood has already passed through the tissues and is returning to the heart.

The correct answer is Option C. Arteries are blood vessels that carry oxygenated blood away from the heart to various parts of the body. Blood pressure is high in arteries during systole when the heart contracts and pumps blood into the arterial system. The arterial walls are thick and elastic to handle the surge of blood during each heartbeat.

Blood pressure is not highest in capillaries. Capillaries are the smallest and thinnest blood vessels where the exchange of oxygen, nutrients, and waste products occurs between the blood and surrounding tissues. Capillaries have thin walls and a large total cross-sectional area, resulting in lower blood pressure compared to arteries and veins.

questions.explanation: Blood pressure is the pressure exerted by the blood on the walls of the blood vessels. The aorta is the largest artery of the body that carries oxygenated blood from the left ventricle of the heart to the rest of the body. Blood pressure is highest in the aorta because this is the location where the blood is ejected from the heart with the greatest force. As the blood moves away from the heart, the blood pressure gradually decreases

questions.text: Albumins constitute _ % of plasma proteins that help in maintaining the colloid osmotic pressure of blood.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Albumins constitute 10% of the plasma proteins. Albumins are a type of protein found in the blood plasma that helps maintain the colloid osmotic pressure of blood. However, 10% is not the correct percentage for albumins.
Albumins constitute 20% of the plasma proteins. This means that albumins make up one-fifth of the total plasma proteins. However, 20% is not the correct percentage for albumins.

Albumins constitute 25% of the plasma proteins. This means that albumins make up one-fourth of the total plasma proteins. 25% is a closer approximation to the correct percentage, but not exactly the correct answer.
Albumins constitute 55% of the plasma proteins. This means albumins comprise more than half of the total plasma proteins. However, 55% is not the correct percentage for albumins.

Albumins constitute around 75% of the plasma proteins. They are the smallest of the plasma proteins and play an important role in maintaining the osmotic pressure of blood, which is necessary for the proper distribution of fluids between the blood and tissues.

questions.explanation: Albumins constitute around 75% of the plasma proteins. They are the smallest of the plasma proteins and play an important role in maintaining the osmotic pressure of blood, which is necessary for the proper distribution of fluids between the blood and tissues.

questions.text: Which one of the following is hypertonic solution to cells present in it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is the hypertonic solution. In a hypertonic environment, water moves out of the cells towards the area of higher solute concentration, causing the cells to shrink.
Option B is the isotonic solution. Isotonic solutions have equal solute and solvent concentrations compared to the cell's cytoplasm, resulting in no net movement of water.
Option C is the hypotonic solution. In a hypotonic solution, the concentration of solutes outside the cell is lower, causing water to move into the cells, which may lead to cell swelling or bursting.
This option is incorrect as one of the provided options (A) is indeed a hypertonic solution.

questions.explanation: Option A is the hypertonic solution, as it causes the cells to shrink due to the movement of water out of the cell towards the higher solute concentration. Option B is the isotonic solution, and option C is the hypotonic solution as it causes the cells to fill with water due to the movement of water towards the lower solute concentration.

questions.text: The soluble part of the blood is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Karyolymph is the fluid portion of the nucleus of a cell. It is made up of water, salts, and dissolved proteins. Karyolymph does not contain any organelles, such as chromosomes or ribosomes.
Nucleoplasm is a gel-like substance within the nucleus containing chromatin. Involved in nuclear processes but not associated with the soluble part of the blood.

Protoplasm is the living material that makes up cells. It is made up of water, proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, and nucleic acids. Protoplasm is not soluble in blood because it is a complex mixture of molecules that are held together by bonds.

The soluble part of the blood is called serum. Serum is the liquid portion of blood that remains after blood clotting and the removal of blood cells, fibrinogen, and clotting factors during the process of coagulation.

questions.explanation: The soluble part of the blood is called serum. Serum is the liquid portion of blood that remains after blood clotting and the removal of blood cells, fibrinogen, and clotting factors during the process of coagulation. It contains various proteins, electrolytes, hormones, antibodies, and other substances that are dissolved in the plasma. Serum is often used in medical testing to analyze different components present in the blood, as it provides valuable information about a person's health and physiological status.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oxygenated blood is bright red because of the presence of oxygen. Oxygen binds to hemoglobin, a protein in red blood cells, and gives the blood its red color.

Deoxygenated blood is darker red or even blue because it has less oxygen. When oxygen leaves the blood, carbon dioxide binds to hemoglobin instead. Carbon dioxide makes the blood appear darker because it absorbs more light.

Blood containing CO2 is reddish purple in color. This is because CO2 binds to hemoglobin, a protein in red blood cells, and changes its shape. The change in shape makes the blood appear darker and more purple. Blood that is high in oxygen is bright red. As blood travels through the body and delivers oxygen to the tissues, it picks up CO2 as a waste product. The more CO2 that is in the blood, the darker it will appear.

Blood containing CO2 can also appear reddish blue. This is because the amount of carbon dioxide in the blood can vary depending on factors such as exercise and breathing. When the body is working harder, it produces more carbon dioxide, which makes the blood appear darker.

questions.explanation: Blood containing carbon dioxide (CO2) is reddish purple in color. This is because CO2 binds to hemoglobin, a protein in red blood cells, and changes its shape. The change in shape makes the blood appear darker and more purple. Blood that is high in oxygen is bright red. As blood travels through the body and delivers oxygen to the tissues, it picks up CO2 as a waste product. The more CO2 that is in the blood, the darker it will appear. Overall, blood containing CO2 is reddish purple in color. The darker the blood, the more CO2 it contains.
